name: Rialb Reservoir Loop from Oliana
name_original: Embalse de Rialb - Vuelta circular desde Oliana
description: This moderate 30.3-mile (48.7 km) road cycling route from Oliana offers fantastic views of the Embalse de Rialb and significant climbs.
region_intro: Road cycling around Oliana offers diverse routes through a pre-Pyrenean landscape in Catalonia, Spain. The region is characterized by the Segre valley, with the town situated between the Oliana and Rialb Reservoirs. Cyclists encounter varied terrain, including hills and scenic views of the Lleida plain. The area provides a network of routes suitable for different fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Oliana The most popular road cycling route is Rialb Reservoir Loop from Oliana, a 30.2 miles (48.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Rialb Reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Peramola – La Clua loop from Oliana, a difficult 47.3 miles (76.2 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through the pre-Pyrenean landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Peramola – Start of the Spring Trail loop from Oliana, a 25.5 miles (41.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 6 minutes. Road cycling around Oliana is defined by its pre-Pyrenean landscape, the Segre valley, and the presence of reservoirs and hills. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Oliana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1,100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Oliana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes around Oliana?
A: Oliana offers a diverse range of road cycling routes. You'll find options for various skill levels, with 17 moderate routes and 27 difficult routes. There is also one easy route available, making it possible for most cyclists to find a suitable challenge.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Oliana?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate to difficult, there is one easy route available. Additionally, the broader Catalonia region features 'Vies Verdes' (Greenway trails), which are converted railway lines known for their gentle gradients, making them accessible for cyclists of all ages and abilities. For a moderate introduction to the area, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38175672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Clua loop from Oliana&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 27.2 km with a manageable elevation gain.

Q: What kind of landscapes and scenic views can I expect while road cycling near Oliana?
A: Road cycling around Oliana offers stunning pre-Pyrenean landscapes. You'll experience the Segre valley, with routes often passing between the beautiful Oliana and Rialb Reservoirs. Cyclists can enjoy scenic views of the Lleida plain and the surrounding mountains. A route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943310947/rialb-reservoir-loop-from-oliana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rialb Reservoir Loop from Oliana&lt;/a&gt; provides excellent views of the reservoir itself.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: The region around Oliana is rich in natural and historical sights. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3622199" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cyclist sculpture at Coll de Serra Seca&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic mountain views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4689400" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfalls of Organyà&lt;/a&gt; provide a refreshing stop. For historical interest, look out for the ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3130203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Sant Salvador&lt;/a&gt;, an 11th-century Romanesque hermitage built into a rock formation, or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3130202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Masía del Corb Cave House&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced road cyclists in the Oliana area?
A: Absolutely. Oliana is well-suited for experienced cyclists, with 27 routes classified as difficult. These routes often feature significant elevation gains through the pre-Pyrenean landscape. For a demanding ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20154164" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peramola – La Clua loop from Oliana&lt;/a&gt;, a 76.2 km path with substantial climbing.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Oliana?
A: The broader Catalonia region, including Oliana, boasts a favorable year-round climate for cycling. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for longer rides, avoiding the peak heat of summer. However, with proper planning, cycling is enjoyable in all seasons.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available around Oliana?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Oliana are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943310947/rialb-reservoir-loop-from-oliana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rialb Reservoir Loop from Oliana&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peramola – Start of the Spring Trail loop from Oliana&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Oliana?
A: The road cycling routes in Oliana are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 1,100 road cyclists have explored the area using komoot, often praising the varied terrain and the scenic pre-Pyrenean landscape.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Oliana region?
A: There are 45 road cycling routes available around Oliana, offering a wide variety of distances and difficulty levels to suit different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a good balance of distance and elevation for a moderate ride?
A: For a moderate ride that offers a good balance, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31865549" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Madrona loop from Oliana&lt;/a&gt;. This route covers 57.2 km with an elevation gain of approximately 845 meters, providing a satisfying challenge without being overly strenuous.
tips: The Castle of the Clua is a building in Bassella (Alt Urgell) declared a Cultural Asset of National Interest. The town is located between the gorge of the Clua, enter cliffs!
Road with beautiful views of the Rialb reservoir. The road winds along the banks of the Segre River.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Komoot classifies this as a moderate road ride requiring good fitness. However, due to its significant elevation gain (around 815 meters) and six main climbs, some sources describe it as a more difficult ride. It's suitable even for experienced cyclists looking for a challenge.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features a mix of surfaces. While it generally has well-paved roads, there are notable "gravel segments." For optimal comfort and performance, a gravel bike or a road bike equipped with thicker tires is recommended.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll be rewarded with fantastic views throughout. Key scenic highlights include beautiful views of the Rialb Reservoir and sections where the road winds along the banks of the Segre River. You'll also pass by the historic La Clua.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting from Oliana. You can typically find parking within Oliana itself to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The trail research doesn't specify a best time, but generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for cycling in Catalonia, avoiding the extreme heat of summer or potential chill of winter. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, cycling with a dog on a leash on roads can be dangerous. It's not typically considered a dog-friendly activity for this type of route.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends in Oliana, you can find various cafes, pubs, and accommodation options within the town. There are also restaurants available for pre- or post-ride meals.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate to difficult rating, significant elevation gain, and mixed terrain including gravel segments, this route is generally not recommended for families with young children or beginner cyclists. It requires good fitness and some cycling experience.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of official trails such as Sender Històric: Catalunya, Pista de Bassella a la Clua, and GR 1.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and elevation, bring plenty of water, snacks, and appropriate cycling gear. Consider a repair kit for punctures, especially if you're tackling the gravel sections. Sun protection is also advisable.
